 {"id":"bd-28h","title":"ext4: Implement CRC32C checksum verification (superblock + inode + dir tail)","description":"Goal: verify ext4 metadata checksums when metadata_csum feature is enabled.

Deliverables:
- Add crc32c dependency (or equivalent) in appropriate crate.
- Implement verify_superblock_checksum() (note: superblock checksum rules differ from group desc).
- Implement inode checksum verification hook (phased).
- Implement directory block tail checksum verification hook (phased).

Acceptance:
- Unit tests: checksum passes on known-good fixture bytes, fails on flipped bit.
- Scrub pipeline can reuse these hooks to detect corruption.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:52.406901062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:06:32.714655569Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:06:32.714637305Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","ondisk"]}
 {"id":"bd-2917w","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_btrfs_xattr_items target for parse_xattr_items","description":"ffs_btrfs::parse_xattr_items (crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs:294) accepts &[u8] from untrusted on-disk data but has no fuzz coverage.

Add to fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_btrfs_tree_items.rs or create a dedicated target:
```rust
let xattr_items = parse_xattr_items(data);
assert_eq!(xattr_items, parse_xattr_items(data), "deterministic");
if let Ok(parsed) = &xattr_items {
    // assert invariants: name_len + data_len match parsed.name.len() + parsed.data.len()
}
```

Acceptance: fuzz target passes cargo fuzz build and exercises parse_xattr_items with invariant checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:33:18.663236318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:21:45Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:21:45Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-29n8t","title":"ext4+btrfs pwritev2 RWF_NOWAIT mounted-path parity proof","description":"Queue-empty porting-to-rust fallback. Add mounted FUSE conformance coverage proving accepted pwritev2 RWF_NOWAIT writes preserve normal caller-offset semantics on ext4 and btrfs when the host/kernel forwards the flag; update FEATURE_PARITY evidence.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-26T02:49:06.887889164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:55:47.070526998Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T02:55:47.068674130Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 and btrfs mounted-path RWF_NOWAIT uncontended offset-write coverage; updated FEATURE_PARITY evidence; validated fmt, UBS, RCH check/clippy/tests/bench.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ext4","fuse","parity","porting"]}
 {"id":"bd-29o","title":"Track: Filesystem Semantics (alloc, btree, extent, inode, dir, xattr, journal)","description":"Implement the actual filesystem metadata/data operations (initially ext4 read-only path) on top of on-disk parsing + block I/O. This is where we reach meaningful parity beyond 'can parse a superblock'.\n\nAcceptance: can resolve paths, read directory entries, read file data (extents), and report metadata via harness; later phases add writes + journal/MVCC integration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:13.171667672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:44:02.965848077Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:44:02.965826407Z","close_reason":"Read-only acceptance criteria met (lookup/readdir/read/xattr/htree/symlink). Write path deferred to bd-zge.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29o","depends_on_id":"bd-16k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:06.665935833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-29o","depends_on_id":"bd-23s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:06.747444684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-29o","depends_on_id":"bd-zge","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:06.832473890Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-29o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track is where parsing turns into an actual filesystem.

We split into:
- ext4 Read-Only MVP (lookup/readdir/read) to unlock FUSE mounting and meaningful parity.
- ext4 Advanced (htree, symlink, xattr, etc).
- Write Path epic (alloc/journal/MVCC integration) for later.

All semantics work must be fixture-backed and routed through a minimal FsOps trait.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29uwc","title":"port ext4 reserved GDT online-resize parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:26:18.007838506Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T09:03:06.174851340Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T09:03:06.172757508Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 online-resize reserved-GDT parity with RESIZE_INODE and group-add planning","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-29z","title":"EPIC: btrfs Write Path","description":"# EPIC: btrfs Write Path

## PURPOSE
Enable write operations on btrfs filesystem images through FUSE, completing the dual-filesystem write capability.

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gaps:
- btrfs transaction/delayed-ref/scrub parity

btrfs write path requires:
1. Transaction boundaries (btrfs native transactions + FrankenFS MVCC)
2. Delayed reference counting (extent back-references)
3. COW tree updates (metadata + data)
4. Checksum tree updates
5. Free space tracking
6. FUSE write surface integration

## CURRENT STATE (CODE)
- btrfs read path works for basic images
- Tree walking implemented in ffs-btrfs
- Superblock/header parsing complete
- No write-path primitives exist

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Implement btrfs transaction model compatible with MVCC
2. Implement delayed reference tracking
3. Implement COW B-tree mutation (insert/delete/update)
4. Wire write ops via FsOps and FUSE

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `echo "test" > /mnt/btrfs/newfile.txt` works on btrfs image
2. Changes persist after unmount/remount
3. COW semantics preserved (no in-place overwrites)
4. MVCC conflict detection works for btrfs

## V1 SCOPE LIMITS
- Single-device images only
- No subvolumes initially
- No compression/encryption
- No RAID profiles

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E btrfs RW test (bd-2ju) passes
3. Extent allocation, delayed refs, and transaction model functional
4. COW writes produce correct version chains
5. btrfs write path integrated with MVCC","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:49:33.304814068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:32.044887960Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:05:32.044873263Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fuse","mvcc"],"comments":[{"id":96,"issue_id":"bd-29z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-29z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: format_path
Mapped graveyard sections: 8.16 SSI certification + 5.1 Typestate + 0.15 Tail decomposition
EV score: 10.666666666666666 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: txn_retry_budget=3, read_latency_p99_budget_ms=50
Fallback trigger: read-only mode + strict compatibility checks

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:22Z"}]}

@@ -286,7 +286,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_all_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `btrfs_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_all_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, leading-hole, and all-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
 | FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for write-intent propagation. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. |
-| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_HIPRI` is treated as a harmless priority/cache hint because bit 0 is also `FUSE_WRITE_CACHE`; coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without sync, NOWAIT, append, or unsupported-intent side effects, and mounted-path ext4 plus btrfs `os.pwritev(..., RWF_HIPRI)` coverage now proves accepted calls preserve caller-offset overwrite semantics. `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID` is likewise kept as a metadata write hint despite sharing raw bit 2 with `RWF_SYNC`; unit coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without an accidental full fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file; the Linux-invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` combination rejects with `EINVAL` before EOF lookup or mutation. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, flag-collision hints, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, invalid append/NOAPPEND conflicts, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for `RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_SYNC`, `RWF_HIPRI`, `RWF_APPEND`, `RWF_NOAPPEND`, the invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` pair, and unsupported `RWF_ATOMIC`/`RWF_DONTCACHE` intents, proving sync writes become immediately readable and survive remount on both ext4 and btrfs, and that harmless hints, append/NOAPPEND offset semantics, and unsupported-intent no-mutation contracts are exercised on both formats when the host permits FUSE and forwards the flags. |
+| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_HIPRI` is treated as a harmless priority/cache hint because bit 0 is also `FUSE_WRITE_CACHE`; coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without sync, NOWAIT, append, or unsupported-intent side effects, and mounted-path ext4 plus btrfs `os.pwritev(..., RWF_HIPRI)` coverage now proves accepted calls preserve caller-offset overwrite semantics. `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID` is likewise kept as a metadata write hint despite sharing raw bit 2 with `RWF_SYNC`; unit coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without an accidental full fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held; mounted-path ext4 plus btrfs coverage now proves accepted uncontended `RWF_NOWAIT` calls preserve normal caller-offset overwrite semantics. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file; the Linux-invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` combination rejects with `EINVAL` before EOF lookup or mutation. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, flag-collision hints, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, invalid append/NOAPPEND conflicts, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for `RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_SYNC`, `RWF_HIPRI`, `RWF_NOWAIT`, `RWF_APPEND`, `RWF_NOAPPEND`, the invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` pair, and unsupported `RWF_ATOMIC`/`RWF_DONTCACHE` intents, proving sync writes become immediately readable and survive remount on both ext4 and btrfs, and that harmless hints, NOWAIT uncontended offset writes, append/NOAPPEND offset semantics, and unsupported-intent no-mutation contracts are exercised on both formats when the host permits FUSE and forwards the flags. |
 | FUSE inotify rename + dentry notification lifecycle | `fs/notify/fsnotify.c`, `fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c`, FUSE notify protocol | ✅ | `FrankenFuse` captures a `fuser::Notifier` during blocking, background, and managed mount setup, then issues `FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY` for successfully committed `unlink`, `rmdir`, and both old/new directory entries after `rename`. Plain renames deliberately avoid `FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE`, preserving the kernel/VFS `IN_MOVED_FROM` + `IN_MOVED_TO` cookie-pair contract instead of synthesizing delete/create observations. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now carries mounted-path inotify probes for 100 same-parent renames and a cross-directory rename; both assert non-zero matching cookies and reject `IN_CREATE`, `IN_DELETE`, `IN_Q_OVERFLOW`, and `IN_IGNORED` during plain rename bursts, with the standard CI soft-skip when the current host denies FUSE mounting. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
